field.label: Certificate
field.help: The Nebula host certificate this instance uses. Required to run.

field.label: Trusted CAs
field.help: Additional CAs whose peers this instance accepts. The CA that signed this instance's own certificate is always trusted; leave empty to trust only that one. Select more to accept peers issued by other CAs (cross-CA trust).

field.label: Disconnect on invalid certificate
field.help: Disconnect any tunnel whose certificate becomes invalid (expired or revoked) while the tunnel is active.

field.label: Initiating cert version
field.help: Nebula handshake version to use when initiating a new tunnel (1 = legacy, 2 = post-quantum-resistant).

field.label: Static map DNS cadence
field.help: How often to re-resolve hostnames listed in static_host_map via DNS.

field.label: Static map address family
field.help: Address family to use when re-resolving static_host_map hostnames: ip4, ip6, or ip (either).

field.label: Static map lookup timeout
field.help: DNS lookup timeout for each static_host_map hostname resolution attempt.

field.label: Lighthouse
field.help: Designate this node as a lighthouse (discovery server); lighthouse nodes should not list other lighthouses.

field.label: Serve DNS
field.help: Enable the DNS server on this lighthouse so peers can resolve Nebula hostnames via DNS queries.

field.label: Lighthouse update interval
field.help: How often (in seconds) non-lighthouse nodes send keepalive/update messages to their lighthouses.

field.label: DNS listen address
field.help: IP address the lighthouse DNS server binds to (only used when serve_dns is true).

field.label: DNS port
field.help: UDP port the lighthouse DNS server listens on (only used when serve_dns is true).

field.label: Lighthouse hosts
field.help: Lighthouse nebula IPs this node reports to / queries. Add each as a tag. Leave empty on a lighthouse.

field.label: Advertise addresses
field.help: Extra routable host:port (or host:0) to advertise to lighthouses. Add each as a tag.

field.label: Listen address
field.help: IP address (or hostname) Nebula binds its UDP listener to; "::" listens on all interfaces (IPv4+IPv6).

field.label: Listen port
field.help: UDP port Nebula listens on; 0 lets the OS assign a random port (useful for roaming clients).

field.label: Firewall interfaces
field.help: Interface(s) on which to automatically allow inbound traffic to this instance's UDP listen port. Defaults to WAN; clear to add no firewall rule (e.g. if you manage the rule yourself).

field.label: Listen batch size
field.help: Maximum number of packets to read from the UDP socket in a single syscall (Linux recvmmsg batch size).

field.label: UDP receive buffer
field.help: SO_RCVBUF size in bytes for the UDP socket; leave unset to use the OS default.

field.label: UDP send buffer
field.help: SO_SNDBUF size in bytes for the UDP socket; leave unset to use the OS default.

field.label: Send error on write failure
field.help: Controls when ICMP unreachable errors are sent back on write failures: always, never, or only to private IPs.

field.label: Send error on read failure
field.help: Controls when ICMP unreachable errors are sent back on read failures: always, never, or only to private IPs.

field.label: Socket mark (SO_MARK)
field.help: SO_MARK value to set on the UDP socket; useful for policy-based routing (0 = disabled).

field.label: Hole punching
field.help: Send UDP hole-punching packets to peers behind NAT to keep the path open.

field.label: Respond to hole punch
field.help: Respond to hole-punch requests from peers (enables bidirectional hole-punching).

field.label: Hole punch delay
field.help: How long to wait before sending the first hole-punch packet after a handshake.

field.label: Hole punch respond delay
field.help: Delay before responding to a hole-punch request; adds jitter to avoid thundering-herd.

field.label: Cipher
field.help: Encryption cipher for tunnel traffic: chachapoly (ChaCha20-Poly1305) or aes (AES-256-GCM).

field.label: Preferred ranges
field.help: Local network CIDRs preferred for handshakes. Add each as a tag.

field.label: Act as relay
field.help: Act as a relay node, forwarding encrypted traffic between peers that cannot reach each other directly.

field.label: Use relays
field.help: Allow this node to use relay nodes when a direct path to a peer is unavailable.

field.label: Relays
field.help: Nebula IPs to use as relays. Add each as a tag.

field.label: Disable TUN interface
field.help: Disable the TUN interface entirely (node participates in the overlay as a relay/lighthouse only, no IP traffic).

field.label: Drop local broadcast
field.help: Drop packets destined for the local broadcast address of the Nebula subnet before they enter the TUN.

field.label: Drop multicast
field.help: Drop all multicast packets at the TUN interface.

field.label: TX queue length
field.help: Transmit queue length for the TUN device (Linux txqueuelen / number of packets buffered before dropping).

field.label: MTU
field.help: MTU of the Nebula TUN interface; lower values leave headroom for the outer UDP/IP headers.

field.label: Use system route table
field.help: Install Nebula routes into the system routing table instead of handling them internally (useful on BSD/macOS).

field.label: System route table buffer size
field.help: Size of the route-change notification buffer when use_system_route_table is enabled; 0 = OS default.

field.label: Drop inactive tunnels
field.help: Tear down tunnels that have had no traffic for the inactivity_timeout period.

field.label: Inactivity timeout
field.help: How long a tunnel may be idle before it is considered inactive (only used when drop_inactive is true).

field.label: Log level
field.help: Log verbosity level: debug produces the most output; panic the least.

field.label: Log format
field.help: Log output format: text (human-readable) or json (structured, for log aggregators).

field.label: Disable log timestamp
field.help: Omit the timestamp field from log entries (useful when the log collector adds its own timestamp).

field.label: Log timestamp format
field.help: Go time-format string for log timestamps (e.g. 2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00); leave empty for the default.

field.label: Default outbound action
field.help: Default action for outbound packets that match no firewall rule: drop (silently) or reject (send ICMP unreachable).

field.label: Default inbound action
field.help: Default action for inbound packets that match no firewall rule: drop (silently) or reject (send ICMP unreachable).

field.label: Default local CIDR = any
field.help: When true, firewall rules without an explicit local_cidr match any local address (pre-v1.9 behaviour).

field.label: Conntrack TCP timeout
field.help: Idle timeout for TCP conntrack entries; connections with no matching traffic are evicted after this period.

field.label: Conntrack UDP timeout
field.help: Idle timeout for UDP conntrack entries.

field.label: Conntrack default timeout
field.help: Idle timeout for conntrack entries that are neither TCP nor UDP.

field.label: Routines
field.help: Number of goroutine workers for packet I/O; increasing can improve throughput on multi-core hosts.

field.label: Handshake retry interval
field.help: How long to wait between successive handshake attempts to the same peer.

field.label: Handshake retries
field.help: Maximum number of handshake attempts before giving up and waiting for the peer to initiate.

field.label: Handshake query buffer
field.help: Size of the channel buffer for outbound lighthouse queries triggered by pending handshakes.

field.label: Handshake trigger buffer
field.help: Size of the channel buffer that queues packets waiting to trigger a new handshake.

field.label: Remote allow list
field.help: Filter which lighthouse-reported remote addresses this node will try. One 'CIDR = true|false' per line; the most specific match wins. Example: 10.0.0.0/8 = false then 10.42.42.0/24 = true.

field.label: Local allow list
field.help: Filter which local addresses are advertised to lighthouses. One 'CIDR = true|false' per line. To match by interface name, write 'interface <regex> = true|false' (rendered under the interfaces: key). Example: 10.0.0.0/8 = true / interface docker.* = false.

field.label: Remote allow ranges (experimental)
field.help: Experimental. Per-VPN-network remote allow lists. One '<vpn-cidr> <remote-cidr> = true|false' per line. Example: 10.42.42.0/24 192.168.0.0/16 = true.

field.label: Calculated remotes (experimental)
field.help: Experimental. Derive a remote address for hosts you have no lighthouse for. One '<vpn-cidr> <mask-cidr> <port>' per line; repeat the vpn-cidr for multiple entries. Example: 192.168.1.0/24 192.168.1.0/24 4242.

field.label: Stats type
field.help: Export Nebula runtime metrics. 'graphite' pushes metrics to a Graphite/Carbon server; 'prometheus' exposes an HTTP endpoint to be scraped. Leave Disabled for no telemetry. The fields below apply only to the selected type.

field.label: Interval
field.help: How often metrics are collected/emitted, e.g. 10s. Applies to both types.

field.label: Per-message metrics
field.help: Emit detailed per-message-type counters (higher cardinality). Applies to both types; off by default.

field.label: Lighthouse metrics
field.help: Emit per-lighthouse metrics. Applies to both types; off by default.

field.label: Graphite: prefix
field.help: Graphite metric-name prefix, e.g. nebula. Used only when type is graphite.

field.label: Graphite: protocol
field.help: Transport used to reach the Graphite server. Used only when type is graphite.

field.label: Graphite: host
field.help: Graphite/Carbon server as host:port, e.g. 127.0.0.1:9999. Required when type is graphite.

field.label: Prometheus: listen
field.help: Address to expose the metrics endpoint on, as host:port, e.g. 127.0.0.1:8080. Required when type is prometheus.

field.label: Prometheus: path
field.help: HTTP path for the metrics endpoint, e.g. /metrics. Used only when type is prometheus.

field.label: Prometheus: namespace
field.help: Prometheus metric namespace. Used only when type is prometheus.

field.label: Prometheus: subsystem
field.help: Prometheus metric subsystem. Used only when type is prometheus.

field.label: Advanced (raw YAML)
field.help: Raw nebula.yml fragment appended to the generated config, for knobs not yet in the GUI (e.g. lighthouse allow-lists). Must be valid YAML and must NOT redefine a top-level key already emitted above (nebula rejects duplicate keys). Validated on Apply via 'nebula -test'.
